scaled_oracle_cases.description: Replay the canonical main() seed flow for compute_probs, verifying core filtering and probability computation.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must produce correct filtered values and probabilities for a typical input with multiple fragment lengths and varying counts.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The solution must correctly apply both the minimum read count threshold (>=100) and the median absolute deviation threshold (<= med + 10*MAD), and output probabilities summing to 1.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: solution fails to filter correctly or miscalculates probabilities for the base case.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
values, probs = compute_probs(datalist)
print("Selected fragment lengths:", values)
print("Their probabilities:", probs)
result = {'values': values, 'probs': probs}
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must provide stronger executable verification coverage for this operator.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify that fragment lengths above the median absolute deviation threshold are correctly excluded even if they have sufficient read support.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must correctly exclude a fragment length that is more than 10 median absolute deviations above the median, despite having high read count.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The condition 'do not lie more than 10 median absolute deviations above the median' must be strictly enforced; outliers with high counts must be excluded.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: solution includes the outlier because it only checks read count threshold or miscomputes MAD.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
values, probs = compute_probs(datalist)
print("Selected fragment lengths:", values)
print("Their probabilities:", probs)
result = {'values': values, 'probs': probs}
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new anti-shortcut robustness requirement introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
